title: Supplement for residents of small and rural communities – Canada Carbon Rebate (CCR) for individuals - Canada.ca
content:
Closed - Canada Carbon Rebate (CCR) for individuals

## Closed: Supplement for residents of small and rural communities
The Canada Carbon Rebate (CCR) included a 20% supplement for residents of small and rural communities.
The supplement applied only to residents of Alberta, Saskatchewan, Manitoba, Ontario, Newfoundland and Labrador, New Brunswick and Nova Scotia whose primary residence was outside a Census Metropolitan Area (CMA), and they expected to continue to reside outside the same CMA on April 1, 2024 .
All CCR recipients of Prince Edward Island were eligible for the rural supplement and it was included in their basic amount.
Information about what defines and composes a CMA: Census Metropolitan Area definition .

## Determine if you qualified for the rural supplement
Using the 2016 Census provincial tables below:
- Find the CMA closest to your primary residence
- Use the corresponding list of municipalities and map to find your primary residence You can zoom in to the map to view street address details
- You can zoom in to the map to view street address details
- Based on where you lived you either: Qualified for the supplement, if your primary residence was located outside all listed municipalities or outside the shaded area of a map, you were considered to be residing outside a CMA and you qualified for the supplement. You would tick the box on page 2 of your income tax and benefit return. Did not qualify for the supplement, if your primary residence was located within a listed municipality or within the shaded area, you were considered to live within a CMA. You would not tick the box on page 2 of your income tax and benefit return.
Based on where you lived you either:
- Qualified for the supplement, if your primary residence was located outside all listed municipalities or outside the shaded area of a map, you were considered to be residing outside a CMA and you qualified for the supplement. You would tick the box on page 2 of your income tax and benefit return.

You would tick the box on page 2 of your income tax and benefit return.
- Did not qualify for the supplement, if your primary residence was located within a listed municipality or within the shaded area, you were considered to live within a CMA. You would not tick the box on page 2 of your income tax and benefit return.

You would not tick the box on page 2 of your income tax and benefit return.
